docs.title: Welcome to Ecommerce Retention Growth
docs.text: This repository contains the full end-to-end Machine Learning pipeline for predicting 30-day subscription churn.

docs.title: 🌟 The Challenge
docs.text:
Subscription services live and die by their retention rates. When customers leave, marketing teams often scramble to offer discounts. But blanket discounts waste money when given to users who would have stayed anyway.

Can we predict who is going to leave, before they leave, and segment them by value?

docs.title: 🚀 Our Solution
docs.text:
By leveraging machine learning and customer segmentation, we target intervention budgets strictly on users passing minimum ROI thresholds.

  • Check out the Data Engineering section to see how we handled 400M+ event logs.
  • See our Model Performance for deeper insights into the XGBoost architecture.
  • Dive into our Business Impact for the actual financial ROI.

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
├── data/                  # Gitignored raw and processed CSVs
├── docs/                  # MkDocs GitHub Pages documentation 
├── figures/               # Automatically generated model evaluation charts
├── notebooks/             # Exploratory analysis and business scenario modeling
├── src/                   # Core Python application logic and pipelines
├── tests/                 # Execution tests via PyTest
├── mkdocs.yml             # Global MkDocs configuration
└── README.md              # Root documentation

docs.title: Business Impact & ROI
docs.text: Predictive modeling is only as valuable as the business decisions it enables.

docs.title: The Problem with Blanket Campaigns
docs.text:
When marketing teams notice an uptick in churn, the typical response is to send out a 20% off coupon to all inactive members. However, many of those users would have renewed at full price anyway.

We lose margin on the "Loyalists" to save the "Flight Risks."

docs.title: Targeted ML-Driven Campaigns
docs.text: This project solves this by scoring every customer with a Churn Probability between 0.0 and 1.0.

docs.title: Simulation Results
docs.text: Below is the comparison of a simulated retention strategy targeting the entire user base versus targeting only users identified by our model as high-risk within high-lifetime-value clusters.

docs.title: The Strategy Breakdown
docs.text:
1. Segment Users: We use K-Means on historic billing and engagement to find our top tier users (Whales).
  2. Predict Churn Risk: We apply our XGBoost classifier to get a 30-day flight risk probability.
  3. Targeted Intervention: Only users who are in the Top 20% of probability and belong to high profitability clusters receive the expensive marketing intervention.

Impact: The ML-driven strategy creates a significant gap in wasted spend, fundamentally raising the ceiling on marketing ROI.

docs.title: Data Engineering & Feature Engineering
docs.text: The pipeline uses the full WSDM KKBox Churn Prediction Challenge dataset. This dataset is immense and requires careful data engineering to feed our modeling pipeline without introducing data leakage.

docs.title: Dataset Volume
docs.text: File Records Description train.csv 970,960 Official ground-truth churn labels (msno, is_churn) members.csv 6,769,473 User demographics (city, age, gender, registration date) transactions.csv ~21M Billing history (plans, payments, auto-renew, cancellations) user_logs.csv ~400M Daily listening activity (songs played, listening time)

docs.title: Pipeline Flow
docs.text:
1. Extraction: Raw data is unzipped locally or read via the Kaggle API. Note: Due to its sheer volume (30GB+), we implemented a chunked sampling methodology in src/sample_kaggle_data.py.
  2. Transform: We join the members demographic data with aggregated metrics from both transactions and user_logs.
  3. Feature Engineering:
  4. Built a Python port of the original Scala labeler.
  5. Designed Recency, Frequency, Monetary (RFM) components from .csv billing history.
  6. Examined 30/60 day rolling engagement trends from user_logs.
  7. Leakage Protection: Strict cutoff mechanisms were established so that only historical data strictly prior to the label month is used.

docs.title: Pipeline Diagram
docs.text:
graph TD;
    A[user_logs.csv] --> C(Aggregations)
    B[transactions.csv] --> C(Aggregations)
    D[members.csv] --> C
    C --> E[Cutoff & Leakage Filter]
    E --> F[Feature Store]
    F --> G[XGBoost Train/Predict]

After extraction and feature engineering, 484,496 labeled users possessed sufficient activity data for training. An additional 205,338 unlabeled users receive predictive scoring.

docs.title: Model Performance
docs.text: We developed several models to establish a robust baseline before pushing specialized gradient boosting frameworks.

docs.title: Performance Metrics
docs.text:
Evaluated on an 80/20 holdout of 484k engineered, labeled users.

Metric Logistic Regression XGBoost Improvement LogLoss 0.6153 0.4802 22.0% better ROC-AUC 0.7264 0.8411 15.8% better PR-AUC 0.2749 0.5157 87.6% better Brier Score 0.2065 0.1608 22.1% better

Note: LogLoss is the official Kaggle competition metric.

docs.title: Interpretability
docs.text: We rely on SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to guarantee that our model isn’t just a black box. This is crucial for stakeholder trust.

docs.title: Global Drivers of Churn
docs.text:
High importance features include:

  • days_since_last_login: Strongest predictor of impending cancellation.
  • auto_renew_status: Users manually renewing have entirely different behavioral patterns.
  • monthly_listening_hours: A primary proxy for product value realizion.

docs.title: Confusion Matrix
docs.text: By thresholding the predicted probability at 0.5, we can observe the classification balance.
